宇須多岐比女命
不足留比女命
菅原道真 |社格 = |創建 = |本殿 = 三間社造銅板葺 |例祭 = [[10月10日]] |文化財 = |ISO = JP-29 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 40|緯度秒 = 21.05 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 49|経度秒 = 45.00 |地図 = Nara city }} '''飛鳥神社'''（あすかじんじゃ）は、[[奈良県]][[奈良市]][[北京終町]]にある[[神社]]。一般に'''京終天神社'''、他に'''紅梅殿神社'''、'''平城坐飛鳥神社'''とも呼ばれる{{sfn|奈良市史 社寺編|page=147}}{{sfn|奈良県史 5 神社|page=268}}。 == 祭神 == 奈良市史によると祭神は以下の五柱{{sfn|奈良市史 社寺編|page=147}}であるが、異説もある{{Refnest|group="注釈"|境内に残る木製の由緒案内板の記載によると、五柱の神は * [[明日香三比女命]] * 天之事代主命 * 賀夜奈留美命 * 宇須太岐比女命 * 贈太政大臣道眞公 となっている。境内設置の木製案内板}}。 * [[事代主神]] * [[カヤナルミ|加夜奈留美命]] * [[宇須多岐比女命]] * [[不足留比女命]] * [[菅原道眞]] == 歴史 == === 由緒 === [[1953年]]（[[昭和]]28年）に[[宗教法人法]]に基づき登記された証書添付の「由緒沿革を示す書類」には、以下のように由緒が示されている由緒沿革を示す書類。 {{Quotation|[[養老]]二年八月{{Refnest|group="注釈"|境内に残る木製の由緒案内板の記載によると、遷宮は[[和銅]]3年（[[711年]]）。}}大和國高市郡飛鳥村の飛鳥神社の御祭神を、平城の新都左京四條七坊飛鳥の丘に、南都[[元興寺]]の[[鎮守社]]として奉遷、鎮祭したことをもって本飛鳥神社の創建となっている。 中世、元興寺の寺運衰退に伴い本神社の機模も沈滞して現況の如くに京終の一隅に移遷{{Refnest|group="注釈"|境内に残る木製の由緒案内板の記載によると、京終への遷宮は[[宝徳]]（[[15世紀]]）[[天文 (元号)|天文]]（[[16世紀]]）の[[土一揆]]で荒廃ののちとされ、同じく境内に残る紙製の案内板の記載では、[[応安]]2年（[[1369年]]）の京終遷宮としている境内設置の紙製案内板。奈良市史も応安2年説を採用している{{sfn|奈良市史 社寺編|page=147}}。}}されることになった。 [[文政]]九年に贈太政大臣道眞公を増祀されて以来何日とはなく紅梅殿の社と呼ばれる習となり、元興寺別当[[大乗院 (門跡寺院)|大乗院]]の院主が代々奉斎していたもので、明治年代に到り、紅梅殿神社として奈良市京終地区一帯の氏神となり、今日に及んだのであるが、旧宗教法人令による紅梅殿神社を解散して、元の由緒ある社名を復元して、飛鳥神社となって今回宗教法人法による宗教法人を設立することになった。}} この登記以降に発行された奈良市史、および奈良県史などの記載でも当神社の歴史は上記由緒に沿う形の記載となっている{{sfn|奈良市史 社寺編|page=147}}{{sfn|奈良県史 5 神社|page=268}}。 === 近世資料での記述と祭神の変化 === しかしながら、登記以前の近世以前資料において、当神社が元興寺と共に飛鳥から移ったとする記述は存在しない。また、由緒では文政9年（[[1826年]]）とされる天神（菅原道眞）の勧請も、古資料では[[17世紀]]に京終村が近隣村落との水論に勝ったことを祝い勧請したとされる記録があり、登記の記述とは矛盾する{{sfn|角川地名大辞典|page=397}}。 古文書、絵図などでの京終村の神社の記述には、社名や祭神などに下記の表のように異同が見られる。 {| class="wikitable" |+ 京終村の神社の記録一覧 ! 資料名 !! 発行時期 !! 神社名 !! 祭神 !! 備考 |- | style="width:15%" | [[村井古道#長闇堂記|長闇堂記]]{{sfn|小堀遠州の作事|page=150,158}} | style="width:15%" | [[寛永]]17年（[[1640年]]） | style="width:15%" | 京終町天神 | style="width:15%" | 菅原道眞社名より推測。 | style="width:40%" | 京終町天神の車除けとして設置されていた仏の彫り込まれていた石を[[久保利世|長闇堂]]が取りおいていたところ、[[小堀政一|小堀遠州]]が気に入って[[灯籠|石灯篭]]の竿として用いて伏見の邸宅に据えたのち、[[徳川秀忠]]に寄贈したとの記録がある{{sfn|小堀遠州の作事|page=150,158}}。
それをきっかけに、竿に仏を彫り込んだ石灯篭が遠州好として流行した{{sfn|小堀遠州の作事|page=150,158}}。 |- | [[奈良曝]]{{sfn|奈良曝|page=39}} || [[貞享]]4年（[[1687年]]） || 富士権現 || [[神明神社|伊勢]]、[[春日神|春日]]
[[富士信仰|富士権現]] || はじめは伊勢、春日を祀っていたが、[[慶長]]18年（[[1613年]]）[[鹿野園町|鹿野園村]]との水論で利運を得たため富士を勧請{{Refnest|group="注釈"|name="suiron"|この時の訴状が、『[[本光国師日記]]』[[慶長]]19年（[[1614年]]）6月12日の条に見える{{sfn|角川地名大辞典|page=397}}{{sfn|本光国師日記|page=97}}。なお、訴状の上では係争の相手方は高畠村となっている{{sfn|本光国師日記|page=97}}。}}。 |- | 奈良町絵図 十二折り本{{sfn|奈良町展図録|page=1}} || [[宝永]]・[[正徳 (日本)|正徳]]期頃
（[[1704年]]-[[1715年]]） || 春日社 || 春日社名より推測。 || - |- | [[奈良坊目拙解]]{{sfn|奈良坊目拙解|page=33}} || [[享保]]15年（[[1730年]]） || 天神社 || 菅原道眞
富士浅間二座（[[コノハナノサクヤビメ|木花開耶姫]]、[[オオヤマツミ|大山祇神]]） || [[高畑町|高畠村]]との水論で[[北野天神]]に祈願したところ利運を得たため天神を勧請。
[[宝永]]3年（[[1706年]]）[[5月6日 (旧暦)|5月6日]]に当神社は火事に遭い、神体神宝、古縁起など全て失われたとされる。 |- | [[大和名所図絵]]{{sfn|大和名所図会|page=132}} || [[寛政]]3年（[[1791年]]） || 富士権現祠 || 伊勢、春日
富士権現 || はじめは伊勢、春日を祀っていたが、慶長年間に富士権現を勧請したとされる。 |- | 奈良細見図{{sfn|奈良細見図}} || [[1889年]]（[[明治]]22年） || 紅梅デン社 || 菅原道眞絵図中に祭神の記載はないが、[[北菅大臣神社#歴史|紅梅殿]]は菅原道眞の居宅を表すため、菅原道眞と推測される。 || - |- | 實地踏測 奈良市街全図{{sfn|奈良市街全図}} || [[1917年]]（[[大正]]6年） || 紅梅殿神社 || 菅原道眞 || - |- | 宗教法人登記上の由緒 || 1953年（昭和28年） || 飛鳥神社 || 元興寺鎮守飛鳥神社の祭神（詳細の記載なし）
菅原道眞 || 平城遷都時に元興寺鎮守社として飛鳥から遷宮、京終には中世に遷宮。
菅原道眞の勧請は文政9年。 |- | 奈良町風土記{{sfn|奈良町風土記|page=76}} || [[1976年]]（昭和51年） || 紅梅天神社 || 菅原道眞
富士浅間二座（木花開耶姫、大山祇神） || 富士浅間は境内社。
近年飛鳥神社と改名と記される。 |- | 奈良市史{{sfn|奈良市史 社寺編|page=147}} || [[1985年]]（昭和60年） || 飛鳥神社 || 事代主神、加夜奈留美命、宇須多岐比女命、不足留比女命、菅原道眞 || [[1977年]]の宗教法人名簿での確認で飛鳥神社とされる。 |} === 近隣神社での類似の由緒 === 当神社の由緒に語られる「平城京遷都時に、一緒に飛鳥から移ってきた元興寺鎮守社」という伝承は、当神社北方400mほどのところに位置する、[[西新屋町]]の[[率川神社 (奈良市西新屋町)|率川神社]]や[[元興寺町]]の[[白山神社 (奈良市元興寺町)|白山神社]]にも、江戸時代の地誌内の記述という形で残る{{sfn|奈良坊目拙解|p=22,58}}{{sfn|元興寺由来|p=153-155}}。その後これらの神社が京終に移ったと示す資料は存在しない。 == 境内 == [[File:Nara Kyobate Temjin Honden1.jpg|thumb|150px|本殿]] [[File:Kasuga-taisha sanjyuhassho jinja.jpg|thumb|150px|参考:春日大社末社 三十八所神社]] [[File:Kasuganocho, Nara, Nara Prefecture 630-8212, Japan - panoramio - jetsun (4).jpg|thumb|150px|参考:春日大社末社 水谷神社]] === 本殿 === [[春日大社]]の[[末社]]旧殿を移した[[春日移し]]の社殿であるが、どの末社を移したかについては、[[三十八所神社]]を移したとする説{{sfn|奈良県史 8 建築|page=912}}と、[[水谷神社 (奈良市)|水谷神社]]を移したとする説がある{{sfn|奈良市史 社寺編|page=147}}{{sfn|奈良県史 5 神社|page=268}}2018年現在の現況による比較では、柱の位置の特徴などにより三十八所神社に類似する。。移築時期は、水谷神社説に従えば文政9年（1826年）とされる{{sfn|奈良市史 社寺編|page=147}}{{sfn|奈良県史 5 神社|page=268}}。[[三間社造]]で、現在の屋根は銅板葺である{{sfn|奈良市史 社寺編|page=147}}。[[雁字板]]に[[剣巴文]]も見られる{{sfn|奈良市史 社寺編|page=147}}。 === 境内社 === * [[宗像神社]]（祭神：[[市杵島姫命]]） - もと花園[[高台寺]]境内に鎮座していた{{sfn|奈良市史 社寺編|page=147}}{{sfn|奈良県史 5 神社|page=268}}。 * 大国主神社（祭神：[[大国主命]]・[[蛭子命]]） - もと[[元興寺|禅定院（極楽坊）]]境内に鎮座していた{{sfn|奈良市史 社寺編|page=147}}{{sfn|奈良県史 5 神社|page=268}}。 * [[金刀比羅神社]]（祭神：[[大物主命]]） - もと[[小塔院]]境内に鎮座していた{{sfn|奈良市史 社寺編|page=147}}{{sfn|奈良県史 5 神社|page=268}}2018年現在の現況では、金刀比羅神社の祭神は木花開耶姫と示されている。。 * [[月原明神社]] - 通称アミダ池の畔にあった小祠であったが、[[1982年]]（[[昭和]]57年）6月に、京終農家組合によって遷された{{sfn|奈良市史 社寺編|page=147}}。 * [[七弁天社]] - 同じくアミダ池畔から遷された{{sfn|奈良市史 社寺編|page=147}}。 その他、[[崇徳天皇]]を祀る社などもある{{sfn|奈良市史 社寺編|page=147}}。 == 現地情報 == ;所在地 *[[奈良県]][[奈良市]]北京終町45 ;交通アクセス * 最寄バス停：JR[[大和路線]][[奈良駅]]下車後、徒歩約20分 == 脚注 == === 注釈 === {{Reflist|group="注釈"|2}} === 出典 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == {{Commonscat|Kyobate Tenjinsha}} {{osm box|w|362086280}} * {{Cite book|和書|title=奈良県史 5 神社|editor1-first=源太|editor1-last=池田|editor1-link=池田源太|editor2-first=敏和|editor2-last=宮坂|editor2-link=宮坂敏和|editor3-first=|editor3-last=奈良県史編集委員会|publisher=[[名著出版]]|year=1989|isbn=462601335X|ref={{sfnref|奈良県史 5 神社}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|title=奈良県史 8 建築|editor=奈良県史編集委員会|publisher=[[名著出版]]|year=1998|ref={{sfnref|奈良県史 8 建築}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|title=奈良市史 社寺編|editor=奈良市史編集審議会|publisher=[[吉川弘文館]]|year=1985|ref={{sfnref|奈良市史 社寺編}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|title=角川日本地名大辞典 29 奈良県|editor=|publisher=[[角川書店]]|year=1990|ref={{sfnref|角川地名大辞典}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|title=奈良曝（奈良市史編集審議会会報付録）|publisher=奈良市史編集審議会|year=1963|ref={{sfnref|奈良曝}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|title=奈良坊目拙解|author=村井古道|authorlink=村井古道|editor=喜多野徳俊 訳・註|editor-link=喜多野徳俊|publisher=綜芸社|year=1977|ref={{sfnref|奈良坊目拙解}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|title=大日本名所図会. 第1輯 第3編（大和名所図会）|editor=大日本名所図会刊行会|publisher=大日本名所図会刊行会|year=1919|ref={{sfnref|大和名所図会}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|title=小堀遠州の作事|author=森蘊|publisher=奈良国立文化財研究所|year=1966|ref={{sfnref|小堀遠州の作事}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|title=奈良町風土記|author=山田熊夫|authorlink=山田熊夫|publisher=豊住書店|year=1976|ref={{sfnref|奈良町風土記}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|title=奈良町-江戸時代の「観光都市」を巡る（開館89周年記念展図録）|author=天理図書館|publisher=天理図書館|year=2019|ref={{sfnref|奈良町展図録}}}} * {{Cite web|和書|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/952843 |title=大日本仏教全書. 139（本光国師日記）|author=仏書刊行会|publisher=国立国会図書館|accessdate=2019-12-18|ref={{sfnref|本光国師日記}}}} * {{Cite web|和書|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/9906279 |title=奈良細見図 1889年（明治22年）|author=筒井庄八|publisher=国立国会図書館|accessdate=2019-12-17|ref={{sfnref|奈良細見図}}}} * {{Cite web|和書|url=https://meta01.library.pref.nara.jp/opac/repository/repo/138174/#?c=0&m=0&s=0&cv=0&r=0&xywh=-1861%2C-282%2C7790%2C5622 |title=實地踏測 奈良市街全図 1917年（大正6年）|author=和楽路屋|publisher=奈良県立図書情報館|accessdate=2019-12-17|ref={{sfnref|奈良市街全図}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|title=本朝佛法最初南都元興寺由来(大日本佛教全書 寺誌叢書 第2)|publisher=佛書刊行會|year=1913|pages=148-156|ref={{sfnref|元興寺由来}}}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:あすかしんしや}} [[Category:奈良市の神社]] [[Category:春日移し|末]] [[Category:天満宮]] [[Category:鎮守社]] [[Category:元興寺]]

revision.text:
[[commons:Category:Kyobate Tenjinsha]][[ja:飛鳥神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q22117766]] Wikidata name: Asuka Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:47, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[ja:飛鳥神社]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Asuka Shrine (Kyōbate Tenjinsha) |image = File:Nara Kyobate Temjin Honden1.jpg |location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|1=Nara Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131287}}{{ill|Nara (city)|ja|奈良市|1=Nara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q169134}} Hokukyōbatechō 45 |deity = {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神|1=Kotoshironushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8009611}}
{{ill|Kayanarumi no Mikoto|ja|加夜奈留美命}}
{{ill|Usutaki-hime no Mikoto|ja|宇須多岐比女命}}
{{ill|Fusokuru-hime no Mikoto|ja|不足留比女命}}
{{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|菅原道真|1=Sugawara no Michizane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} |rank = |founded = |honden = Sangensha-zukuri, copper roof |annual_festival = {{ill|October 10|ja|10月10日|1=October 10|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2921}} |cultural_property = |ISO = JP-29 |lat_d = 34|lat_m = 40|lat_s = 21.05 |lon_d = 135|lon_m = 49|lon_s = 45.00 |map = Nara city |location = {{ill|Nara|qid=Q169134}} |coordinates = {{Coord|34.67252777777778|N|135.82916666666668|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Asuka Shrine''' (Asuka Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|1=Shinto shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q845945}} located in {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|1=Nara Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131287}}, {{ill|Nara (city)|ja|奈良市|1=Nara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q169134}}, {{ill|Hokukyōbatechō|ja|北京終町}}. It is commonly referred to as '''Kyōbate Tenjinsha''', and also known as '''Kōbaiden Shrine''' and '''Heijōza Asuka Shrine'''{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples Edition|page=147}}{{sfn|Nara Prefectural History 5: Shrines|page=268}}. == Deities == According to the Nara City History, the shrine enshrines the following five kami{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples Edition|page=147}}, although there are alternate traditions{{Refnest|group="Note"|According to a wooden information board on the grounds, the five kami are:
* {{ill|Asuka Sanbime no Mikoto|ja|明日香三比女命}}
* {{ill|Ame no Kotoshironushi|ja|天之事代主命}}
* {{ill|Kayanarumi no Mikoto|ja|賀夜奈留美命}}
* {{ill|Usutaki-hime no Mikoto|ja|宇須太岐比女命}}
* {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|贈太政大臣道眞公|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}Wooden board on-site}}. * {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神|1=Kotoshironushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8009611}} * {{ill|Kayanarumi no Mikoto|ja|加夜奈留美命}} * {{ill|Usutaki-hime no Mikoto|ja|宇須多岐比女命}} * {{ill|Fusokuru-hime no Mikoto|ja|不足留比女命}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|菅原道真|1=Sugawara no Michizane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} == History == === Origins === According to a document titled "Document Showing Shrine Origins and History," attached to the official 1953 registration under the Religious Corporations ActDocument Showing Shrine Origins and History: {{Quotation|In the 8th month of the 2nd year of {{ill|Yōrō|ja|養老|1=Yōrō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1373061}} ({{Refnest|group="Note"|According to a wooden board on the premises, the transfer occurred in {{ill|Wadō 3|ja|和銅3年}} ({{ill|711|ja|711年|1=711|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q23519}}).}}), the enshrined deity of Asuka Shrine in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|飛鳥村|1=Asuka, Nara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11665974}}, Takaichi District, Yamato Province, was transferred to the hill of Asuka in the Shijō Nanabō (4th Avenue, 7th Ward) of the new capital of Heijō, as the tutelary shrine of the {{ill|Gangō-ji|ja|元興寺|1=Gangō-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1203099}} temple in Nanto. This marked the founding of the present Asuka Shrine. In the medieval period, as Gangō-ji fell into decline, the shrine also diminished and was relocated to a corner of Kyōbate, as it remains today.{{Refnest|group="Note"|According to a wooden board on-site, the relocation to Kyōbate occurred after the {{ill|Bunmei uprisings|ja|土一揆|1=tsuchi-ikki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11422932}} during the {{ill|Hōtoku era|ja|宝徳|1=Hōtoku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1143606}} (15th century) or {{ill|Tenbun era|ja|天文|1=Tenbun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21096812}} (16th century). A paper signboard on the premises says it happened in {{ill|Ōan 2|ja|応安2年}} ({{ill|1369|ja|1369年|1=1369|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6211}})Paper board on-site. The Nara City History adopts the Ōan 2 date{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples Edition|page=147}}.}} In {{ill|Bunsei 9|ja|文政9年}}, {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|菅原道真|1=Sugawara no Michizane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} was added as a deity, and the shrine came to be popularly known as Kōbaiden Shrine. The {{ill|Daijōin|ja|大乗院|1=Daijō-in|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16766994}} abbots of Gangō-ji oversaw its worship. In the Meiji period, it became the ujigami (local tutelary shrine) of the Kyōbate area of Nara City under the name Kōbaiden Shrine. However, the old religious corporation under that name was dissolved and the shrine reverted to its historic name of Asuka Shrine, becoming a new religious corporation under the postwar Religious Corporations Act. The Nara City and Prefectural histories issued thereafter follow this narrative{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples Edition|page=147}}{{sfn|Nara Prefectural History 5: Shrines|page=268}}. === Changes in Records and Deities in the Early Modern Period === However, no early modern or earlier records mention that this shrine moved from Asuka with Gangō-ji. Also, while the origin document claims that Tenjin (Sugawara no Michizane) was enshrined in {{ill|Bunsei 9|ja|文政9年}} ({{ill|1826|ja|1826年|1=1826|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7004}}), older records say Tenjin was enshrined to commemorate Kyōbate Village’s victory in a water dispute in the {{ill|17th century|ja|17世紀|1=17th century|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7016}}{{sfn|Kadokawa Dictionary of Place Names|page=397}}. Early documents and maps show various discrepancies in the shrine's name and enshrined deities: {| class="wikitable" |+ Records of Shrines in Kyōbate Village ! Document !! Period !! Shrine Name !! Deity !! Notes |- | {{ill|Nagakuradouki|ja|長闇堂記|1=Murai Kodō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q96711987}}{{sfn|Kobori Enshū’s Architecture|page=150,158}} || {{ill|Kan'ei 17|ja|寛永17年}} ({{ill|1640|ja|1640年|1=1640|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6901}}) || Kyōbate Town Tenjin || {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|菅原道真|1=Sugawara no Michizane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}Inferred from the shrine name. || The carved stone used as a traffic barrier was taken by {{ill|Nagakuradou|ja|長闇堂}} and later admired by {{ill|Kobori Enshū|ja|小堀遠州|1=Kobori Enshū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q541040}}, who used it in a lantern at his villa and gifted it to {{ill|Tokugawa Hidetada|ja|徳川秀忠|1=Tokugawa Hidetada|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q314464}}. This popularized lanterns with Buddhist carvings{{sfn|Kobori Enshū’s Architecture|page=150,158}}. |- | {{ill|Nara Bak|ja|奈良曝}}{{sfn|Nara Bak|page=39}} || {{ill|Jōkyō 4|ja|貞享4年}} ({{ill|1687|ja|1687年|1=1687|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7668}}) || Fuji Gongen || {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|伊勢}}, {{ill|Kasuga|ja|春日|1=Kasuga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q446420}}, {{ill|Fuji Gongen|ja|富士権現}} || Originally enshrined Amaterasu and Kasuga, but Fuji was added after a victory in a water dispute with {{ill|Kanōon Village|ja|鹿野園村}} in {{ill|Keichō 18|ja|慶長18年}} ({{ill|1613|ja|1613年|1=1613|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6776}}){{Refnest|group="Note"|name="suiron"|The legal complaint from that time appears in the {{ill|Honkō Kokushi Diary|ja|本光国師日記|1=Honkō Kokushi Nikki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11519861}}, entry for {{ill|Keichō 19|ja|慶長19年|1=1614|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6781}}, 6th month, 12th day{{sfn|Kadokawa Dictionary of Place Names|page=397}}{{sfn|Honkō Diary|page=97}}. The dispute opponent is listed as Takabatake Village{{sfn|Honkō Diary|page=97}}.}}. |- | Nara Town Map, Twelve-fold book{{sfn|Nara Exhibition Catalog|page=1}} || ca. {{ill|Hōei|ja|宝永|1=Hōei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1145262}}–{{ill|Shōtoku (Japanese era)|ja|正徳|1=正徳|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11123425}} ({{ill|1704–1715|ja|1704年–1715年}}) || Kasuga Shrine || KasugaInferred from name. || - |- | {{ill|Narabōmoku Sakkai|ja|奈良坊目拙解|1=Murai Kodō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q96711987}}{{sfn|Narabōmoku Sakkai|page=33}} || {{ill|Kyōhō 15|ja|享保15年}} ({{ill|1730|ja|1730年|1=1730|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6957}}) || Tenjinsha || {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|菅原道真|1=Sugawara no Michizane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}, {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫}}, {{ill|Ōyama-tsumi|ja|大山祇神|1=Ōyamatsumi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q386563}} || Enshrinement of Tenjin after water dispute victory over Takabatake Village. Shrine destroyed in a fire on {{ill|May 6, 1706|ja|宝永3年5月6日}}, all relics and records lost. |- | {{ill|Yamato Meisho Zue|ja|大和名所図絵}}{{sfn|Yamato Meisho Zue|page=132}} || {{ill|Kansei 3|ja|寛政3年}} ({{ill|1791|ja|1791年|1=1791|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7784}}) || Fuji Gongen Shrine || Ise, Kasuga, Fuji Gongen || Initially Ise and Kasuga, Fuji added during Keichō era. |- | {{ill|Nara Saikenzu|ja|奈良細見図}}{{sfn|Nara Saikenzu}} || {{ill|1889|ja|1889年|1=1889|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7831}} ({{ill|Meiji 22|ja|明治22年|1=1889|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7831}}) || Kōbaiden Shrine || Sugawara no MichizaneInferred from association with Kōbaiden, the residence of Sugawara no Michizane. || - |- | Field Survey Nara City Map{{sfn|Nara City Map}} || {{ill|1917|ja|大正6年|1=1917|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2092}} || Kōbaiden Shrine || Sugawara no Michizane || - |- | Official Registration History || {{ill|1953|ja|昭和28年|1=1953|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18585}} || Asuka Shrine || Gangō-ji’s tutelary deity (unspecified)
Sugawara no Michizane || Transferred from Asuka to Heijō with Gangō-ji; moved to Kyōbate in medieval period. Michizane added in 1826. |- | {{ill|Nara Town Topography|ja|奈良町風土記}}{{sfn|Nara Town Topography|page=76}} || {{ill|1976|ja|昭和51年|1=1976|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2480}} || Kōbai Tenjinsha || Sugawara no Michizane
Konohanasakuya-hime, Ōyama-tsumi || Fuji deities are auxiliary shrines. Recently renamed Asuka Shrine. |- | Nara City History{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples Edition|page=147}} || {{ill|1985|ja|昭和60年|1=1985|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2431}} || Asuka Shrine || Kotoshironushi, Kayanarumi, Usutaki-hime, Fusokuru-hime, Sugawara no Michizane || Listed as Asuka Shrine in 1977 registry. |} === Similar Origins at Nearby Shrines === The tradition that this shrine was the tutelary shrine of Gangō-ji transferred from Asuka during the establishment of Heijō-kyō is also found in Edo-period records regarding nearby shrines such as {{ill|Isagawa Shrine (Nara City Nishishinmachi)|ja|率川神社 (奈良市西新屋町)|1=Isagawa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q48761131}} about 400 meters north in {{ill|Nishishinmachi, Nara|ja|西新屋町|1=Nishinoshin'yachō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28686454}}, and {{ill|Hakusan Shrine (Nara City Gangoji Town)|ja|白山神社 (奈良市元興寺町)|1=Hakusan Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q48761897}} in {{ill|Gangoji Town|ja|元興寺町|1=Gangōji-chō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28687279}}{{sfn|Narabōmoku Sakkai|p=22,58}}{{sfn|Gangoji Origin Record|p=153–155}}. However, no sources confirm these shrines moved to Kyōbate. == Shrine Grounds == [[File:Nara Kyobate Temjin Honden1.jpg|thumb|150px|Main Hall]] [[File:Kasuga-taisha sanjyuhassho jinja.jpg|thumb|150px|Reference: Kasuga Shrine Sub-shrine Sanjūhassho Shrine]] [[File:Kasuganocho, Nara, Nara Prefecture 630-8212, Japan - panoramio - jetsun (4).jpg|thumb|150px|Reference: Kasuga Shrine Sub-shrine Mizutani Shrine]] === Main Hall === The main hall was relocated from a former sub-shrine of {{ill|Kasuga Shrine|ja|春日大社|1=Kasuga-taisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q714559}} in a practice known as “Kasuga Utsushi.” There are two theories about which sub-shrine was moved: one identifies it as the {{ill|Sanjūhassho Shrine|ja|三十八所神社}}{{sfn|Nara Prefectural History 8: Architecture|page=912}}, the other as {{ill|Mizutani Shrine (Nara City)|ja|水谷神社|1=Mizutani Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11549203}}{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples Edition|page=147}}{{sfn|Nara Prefectural History 5: Shrines|page=268}}.As of 2018, structural comparisons such as pillar placement suggest resemblance to Sanjūhassho Shrine. If it is Mizutani Shrine, the relocation is thought to have occurred in {{ill|Bunsei 9|ja|文政9年}} ({{ill|1826|ja|1826年|1=1826|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7004}}){{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples Edition|page=147}}{{sfn|Nara Prefectural History 5: Shrines|page=268}}. The structure is a {{ill|Sangensha-zukuri|ja|三間社造}} with a copper-sheet roof{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples Edition|page=147}}. Decorative elements such as kenbamon (sword-hilt crests) are seen on the lintels{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples Edition|page=147}}. === Sub-shrines === * {{ill|Munakata Shrine|ja|宗像神社|1=Munakata shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11451876}} (Deity: {{ill|Ichikishimahime no Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishimahime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11059454}}) – formerly located within the grounds of {{ill|Kōdaiji (Kyoto)|ja|高台寺|1=Kōdai-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2702064}} in Hanazono{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples Edition|page=147}}{{sfn|Nara Prefectural History 5: Shrines|page=268}}. * Ōkuninushi Shrine (Deities: {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命}}, {{ill|Hiruko|ja|蛭子命|1=Hiruko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1067028}}) – formerly within {{ill|Gokurakubō|ja|禅定院（極楽坊）}} of Gangō-ji{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples Edition|page=147}}{{sfn|Nara Prefectural History 5: Shrines|page=268}}. * {{ill|Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社|1=Kotohira shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11646488}} (Deity: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命}}) – formerly at {{ill|Shōtōin|ja|小塔院|1=Shōtō-in Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11459543}}{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples Edition|page=147}}{{sfn|Nara Prefectural History 5: Shrines|page=268}}.As of 2018, the enshrined deity is indicated as Konohanasakuya-hime. * {{ill|Tsukihara Myōjinsha|ja|月原明神社}} – A small shrine once by the pond called Amida-ike; relocated in {{ill|1982|ja|昭和57年|1=1982|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2436}}, June, by the Kyōbate Agricultural Union{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples Edition|page=147}}. * {{ill|Shichibenten Shrine|ja|七弁天社}} – Also relocated from the bank of Amida-ike{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples Edition|page=147}}. Other enshrined deities include {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|ja|崇徳天皇|1=Emperor Sutoku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q349287}}{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples Edition|page=147}}. == [[sn:commons:Category:Kyobate Tenjinsha]][[ja:飛鳥神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q22117766]] Wikidata name: Asuka Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:47, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[ja:飛鳥神社]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Asuka Shrine (Kyōbate Tenjinsha) |image = File:Nara Kyobate Temjin Honden1.jpg |location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|1=Nara Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131287}}{{ill|Nara (city)|ja|奈良市|1=Nara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q169134}} Hokukyōbatechō 45 |deity = {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神|1=Kotoshironushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8009611}}
{{ill|Kayanarumi no Mikoto|ja|加夜奈留美命}}
{{ill|Usutaki-hime no Mikoto|ja|宇須多岐比女命}}
{{ill|Fusokuru-hime no Mikoto|ja|不足留比女命}}
{{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|菅原道真|1=Sugawara no Michizane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} |rank = |founded = |honden = Sangensha-zukuri, copper roof |annual_festival = {{ill|October 10|ja|10月10日|1=October 10|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2921}} |cultural_property = |ISO = JP-29 |lat_d = 34|lat_m = 40|lat_s = 21.05 |lon_d = 135|lon_m = 49|lon_s = 45.00 |map = Nara city |location = {{ill|Nara|qid=Q169134}} |coordinates = {{Coord|34.67252777777778|N|135.82916666666668|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Asuka Shrine''' (Asuka Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|1=Shinto shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q845945}} located in {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|1=Nara Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131287}}, {{ill|Nara (city)|ja|奈良市|1=Nara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q169134}}, {{ill|Hokukyōbatechō|ja|北京終町}}. It is commonly referred to as '''Kyōbate Tenjinsha''', and also known as '''Kōbaiden Shrine''' and '''Heijōza Asuka Shrine'''{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples Edition|page=147}}{{sfn|Nara Prefectural History 5: Shrines|page=268}}. == Deities == According to the Nara City History, the shrine enshrines the following five kami{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples Edition|page=147}}, although there are alternate traditions{{Refnest|group="Note"|According to a wooden information board on the grounds, the five kami are:
* {{ill|Asuka Sanbime no Mikoto|ja|明日香三比女命}}
* {{ill|Ame no Kotoshironushi|ja|天之事代主命}}
* {{ill|Kayanarumi no Mikoto|ja|賀夜奈留美命}}
* {{ill|Usutaki-hime no Mikoto|ja|宇須太岐比女命}}
* {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|贈太政大臣道眞公|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}Wooden board on-site}}. * {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神|1=Kotoshironushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8009611}} * {{ill|Kayanarumi no Mikoto|ja|加夜奈留美命}} * {{ill|Usutaki-hime no Mikoto|ja|宇須多岐比女命}} * {{ill|Fusokuru-hime no Mikoto|ja|不足留比女命}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|菅原道真|1=Sugawara no Michizane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} == History == === Origins === According to a document titled "Document Showing Shrine Origins and History," attached to the official 1953 registration under the Religious Corporations ActDocument Showing Shrine Origins and History: {{Quotation|In the 8th month of the 2nd year of {{ill|Yōrō|ja|養老|1=Yōrō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1373061}} ({{Refnest|group="Note"|According to a wooden board on the premises, the transfer occurred in {{ill|Wadō 3|ja|和銅3年}} ({{ill|711|ja|711年|1=711|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q23519}}).}}), the enshrined deity of Asuka Shrine in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|飛鳥村|1=Asuka, Nara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11665974}}, Takaichi District, Yamato Province, was transferred to the hill of Asuka in the Shijō Nanabō (4th Avenue, 7th Ward) of the new capital of Heijō, as the tutelary shrine of the {{ill|Gangō-ji|ja|元興寺|1=Gangō-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1203099}} temple in Nanto. This marked the founding of the present Asuka Shrine. In the medieval period, as Gangō-ji fell into decline, the shrine also diminished and was relocated to a corner of Kyōbate, as it remains today.{{Refnest|group="Note"|According to a wooden board on-site, the relocation to Kyōbate occurred after the {{ill|Bunmei uprisings|ja|土一揆|1=tsuchi-ikki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11422932}} during the {{ill|Hōtoku era|ja|宝徳|1=Hōtoku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1143606}} (15th century) or {{ill|Tenbun era|ja|天文|1=Tenbun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21096812}} (16th century). A paper signboard on the premises says it happened in {{ill|Ōan 2|ja|応安2年}} ({{ill|1369|ja|1369年|1=1369|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6211}})Paper board on-site. The Nara City History adopts the Ōan 2 date{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples Edition|page=147}}.}} In {{ill|Bunsei 9|ja|文政9年}}, {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|菅原道真|1=Sugawara no Michizane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} was added as a deity, and the shrine came to be popularly known as Kōbaiden Shrine. The {{ill|Daijōin|ja|大乗院|1=Daijō-in|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16766994}} abbots of Gangō-ji oversaw its worship. In the Meiji period, it became the ujigami (local tutelary shrine) of the Kyōbate area of Nara City under the name Kōbaiden Shrine. However, the old religious corporation under that name was dissolved and the shrine reverted to its historic name of Asuka Shrine, becoming a new religious corporation under the postwar Religious Corporations Act. The Nara City and Prefectural histories issued thereafter follow this narrative{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples Edition|page=147}}{{sfn|Nara Prefectural History 5: Shrines|page=268}}. === Changes in Records and Deities in the Early Modern Period === However, no early modern or earlier records mention that this shrine moved from Asuka with Gangō-ji. Also, while the origin document claims that Tenjin (Sugawara no Michizane) was enshrined in {{ill|Bunsei 9|ja|文政9年}} ({{ill|1826|ja|1826年|1=1826|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7004}}), older records say Tenjin was enshrined to commemorate Kyōbate Village’s victory in a water dispute in the {{ill|17th century|ja|17世紀|1=17th century|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7016}}{{sfn|Kadokawa Dictionary of Place Names|page=397}}. Early documents and maps show various discrepancies in the shrine's name and enshrined deities: {| class="wikitable" |+ Records of Shrines in Kyōbate Village ! Document !! Period !! Shrine Name !! Deity !! Notes |- | {{ill|Nagakuradouki|ja|長闇堂記|1=Murai Kodō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q96711987}}{{sfn|Kobori Enshū’s Architecture|page=150,158}} || {{ill|Kan'ei 17|ja|寛永17年}} ({{ill|1640|ja|1640年|1=1640|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6901}}) || Kyōbate Town Tenjin || {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|菅原道真|1=Sugawara no Michizane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}Inferred from the shrine name. || The carved stone used as a traffic barrier was taken by {{ill|Nagakuradou|ja|長闇堂}} and later admired by {{ill|Kobori Enshū|ja|小堀遠州|1=Kobori Enshū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q541040}}, who used it in a lantern at his villa and gifted it to {{ill|Tokugawa Hidetada|ja|徳川秀忠|1=Tokugawa Hidetada|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q314464}}. This popularized lanterns with Buddhist carvings{{sfn|Kobori Enshū’s Architecture|page=150,158}}. |- | {{ill|Nara Bak|ja|奈良曝}}{{sfn|Nara Bak|page=39}} || {{ill|Jōkyō 4|ja|貞享4年}} ({{ill|1687|ja|1687年|1=1687|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7668}}) || Fuji Gongen || {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|伊勢}}, {{ill|Kasuga|ja|春日|1=Kasuga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q446420}}, {{ill|Fuji Gongen|ja|富士権現}} || Originally enshrined Amaterasu and Kasuga, but Fuji was added after a victory in a water dispute with {{ill|Kanōon Village|ja|鹿野園村}} in {{ill|Keichō 18|ja|慶長18年}} ({{ill|1613|ja|1613年|1=1613|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6776}}){{Refnest|group="Note"|name="suiron"|The legal complaint from that time appears in the {{ill|Honkō Kokushi Diary|ja|本光国師日記|1=Honkō Kokushi Nikki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11519861}}, entry for {{ill|Keichō 19|ja|慶長19年|1=1614|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6781}}, 6th month, 12th day{{sfn|Kadokawa Dictionary of Place Names|page=397}}{{sfn|Honkō Diary|page=97}}. The dispute opponent is listed as Takabatake Village{{sfn|Honkō Diary|page=97}}.}}. |- | Nara Town Map, Twelve-fold book{{sfn|Nara Exhibition Catalog|page=1}} || ca. {{ill|Hōei|ja|宝永|1=Hōei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1145262}}–{{ill|Shōtoku (Japanese era)|ja|正徳|1=正徳|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11123425}} ({{ill|1704–1715|ja|1704年–1715年}}) || Kasuga Shrine || KasugaInferred from name. || - |- | {{ill|Narabōmoku Sakkai|ja|奈良坊目拙解|1=Murai Kodō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q96711987}}{{sfn|Narabōmoku Sakkai|page=33}} || {{ill|Kyōhō 15|ja|享保15年}} ({{ill|1730|ja|1730年|1=1730|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6957}}) || Tenjinsha || {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|菅原道真|1=Sugawara no Michizane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}, {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫}}, {{ill|Ōyama-tsumi|ja|大山祇神|1=Ōyamatsumi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q386563}} || Enshrinement of Tenjin after water dispute victory over Takabatake Village. Shrine destroyed in a fire on {{ill|May 6, 1706|ja|宝永3年5月6日}}, all relics and records lost. |- | {{ill|Yamato Meisho Zue|ja|大和名所図絵}}{{sfn|Yamato Meisho Zue|page=132}} || {{ill|Kansei 3|ja|寛政3年}} ({{ill|1791|ja|1791年|1=1791|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7784}}) || Fuji Gongen Shrine || Ise, Kasuga, Fuji Gongen || Initially Ise and Kasuga, Fuji added during Keichō era. |- | {{ill|Nara Saikenzu|ja|奈良細見図}}{{sfn|Nara Saikenzu}} || {{ill|1889|ja|1889年|1=1889|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7831}} ({{ill|Meiji 22|ja|明治22年|1=1889|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7831}}) || Kōbaiden Shrine || Sugawara no MichizaneInferred from association with Kōbaiden, the residence of Sugawara no Michizane. || - |- | Field Survey Nara City Map{{sfn|Nara City Map}} || {{ill|1917|ja|大正6年|1=1917|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2092}} || Kōbaiden Shrine || Sugawara no Michizane || - |- | Official Registration History || {{ill|1953|ja|昭和28年|1=1953|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18585}} || Asuka Shrine || Gangō-ji’s tutelary deity (unspecified)
Sugawara no Michizane || Transferred from Asuka to Heijō with Gangō-ji; moved to Kyōbate in medieval period. Michizane added in 1826. |- | {{ill|Nara Town Topography|ja|奈良町風土記}}{{sfn|Nara Town Topography|page=76}} || {{ill|1976|ja|昭和51年|1=1976|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2480}} || Kōbai Tenjinsha || Sugawara no Michizane
Konohanasakuya-hime, Ōyama-tsumi || Fuji deities are auxiliary shrines. Recently renamed Asuka Shrine. |- | Nara City History{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples Edition|page=147}} || {{ill|1985|ja|昭和60年|1=1985|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2431}} || Asuka Shrine || Kotoshironushi, Kayanarumi, Usutaki-hime, Fusokuru-hime, Sugawara no Michizane || Listed as Asuka Shrine in 1977 registry. |} === Similar Origins at Nearby Shrines === The tradition that this shrine was the tutelary shrine of Gangō-ji transferred from Asuka during the establishment of Heijō-kyō is also found in Edo-period records regarding nearby shrines such as {{ill|Isagawa Shrine (Nara City Nishishinmachi)|ja|率川神社 (奈良市西新屋町)|1=Isagawa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q48761131}} about 400 meters north in {{ill|Nishishinmachi, Nara|ja|西新屋町|1=Nishinoshin'yachō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28686454}}, and {{ill|Hakusan Shrine (Nara City Gangoji Town)|ja|白山神社 (奈良市元興寺町)|1=Hakusan Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q48761897}} in {{ill|Gangoji Town|ja|元興寺町|1=Gangōji-chō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28687279}}{{sfn|Narabōmoku Sakkai|p=22,58}}{{sfn|Gangoji Origin Record|p=153–155}}. However, no sources confirm these shrines moved to Kyōbate. == Shrine Grounds == [[File:Nara Kyobate Temjin Honden1.jpg|thumb|150px|Main Hall]] [[File:Kasuga-taisha sanjyuhassho jinja.jpg|thumb|150px|Reference: Kasuga Shrine Sub-shrine Sanjūhassho Shrine]] [[File:Kasuganocho, Nara, Nara Prefecture 630-8212, Japan - panoramio - jetsun (4).jpg|thumb|150px|Reference: Kasuga Shrine Sub-shrine Mizutani Shrine]] === Main Hall === The main hall was relocated from a former sub-shrine of {{ill|Kasuga Shrine|ja|春日大社|1=Kasuga-taisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q714559}} in a practice known as “Kasuga Utsushi.” There are two theories about which sub-shrine was moved: one identifies it as the {{ill|Sanjūhassho Shrine|ja|三十八所神社}}{{sfn|Nara Prefectural History 8: Architecture|page=912}}, the other as {{ill|Mizutani Shrine (Nara City)|ja|水谷神社|1=Mizutani Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11549203}}{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples Edition|page=147}}{{sfn|Nara Prefectural History 5: Shrines|page=268}}.As of 2018, structural comparisons such as pillar placement suggest resemblance to Sanjūhassho Shrine. If it is Mizutani Shrine, the relocation is thought to have occurred in {{ill|Bunsei 9|ja|文政9年}} ({{ill|1826|ja|1826年|1=1826|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7004}}){{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples Edition|page=147}}{{sfn|Nara Prefectural History 5: Shrines|page=268}}. The structure is a {{ill|Sangensha-zukuri|ja|三間社造}} with a copper-sheet roof{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples Edition|page=147}}. Decorative elements such as kenbamon (sword-hilt crests) are seen on the lintels{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples Edition|page=147}}. === Sub-shrines === * {{ill|Munakata Shrine|ja|宗像神社|1=Munakata shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11451876}} (Deity: {{ill|Ichikishimahime no Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishimahime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11059454}}) – formerly located within the grounds of {{ill|Kōdaiji (Kyoto)|ja|高台寺|1=Kōdai-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2702064}} in Hanazono{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples Edition|page=147}}{{sfn|Nara Prefectural History 5: Shrines|page=268}}. * Ōkuninushi Shrine (Deities: {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命}}, {{ill|Hiruko|ja|蛭子命|1=Hiruko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1067028}}) – formerly within {{ill|Gokurakubō|ja|禅定院（極楽坊）}} of Gangō-ji{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples Edition|page=147}}{{sfn|Nara Prefectural History 5: Shrines|page=268}}. * {{ill|Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社|1=Kotohira shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11646488}} (Deity: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命}}) – formerly at {{ill|Shōtōin|ja|小塔院|1=Shōtō-in Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11459543}}{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples Edition|page=147}}{{sfn|Nara Prefectural History 5: Shrines|page=268}}.As of 2018, the enshrined deity is indicated as Konohanasakuya-hime. * {{ill|Tsukihara Myōjinsha|ja|月原明神社}} – A small shrine once by the pond called Amida-ike; relocated in {{ill|1982|ja|昭和57年|1=1982|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2436}}, June, by the Kyōbate Agricultural Union{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples Edition|page=147}}. * {{ill|Shichibenten Shrine|ja|七弁天社}} – Also relocated from the bank of Amida-ike{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples Edition|page=147}}. Other enshrined deities include {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|ja|崇徳天皇|1=Emperor Sutoku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q349287}}{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples Edition|page=147}}. == [[sn:commons:Category:Kyobate Tenjinsha]][[ja:飛鳥神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q22117766]] Wikidata name: Asuka Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:47, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[ja:飛鳥神社]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Asuka Shrine (Kyōbate Tenjinsha) |image = File:Nara Kyobate Temjin Honden1.jpg |location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|1=Nara Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131287}}{{ill|Nara (city)|ja|奈良市|1=Nara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q169134}} Hokukyōbatechō 45 |deity = {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神|1=Kotoshironushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8009611}}
{{ill|Kayanarumi no Mikoto|ja|加夜奈留美命}}
{{ill|Usutaki-hime no Mikoto|ja|宇須多岐比女命}}
{{ill|Fusokuru-hime no Mikoto|ja|不足留比女命}}
{{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|菅原道真|1=Sugawara no Michizane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} |rank = |founded = |honden = Sangensha-zukuri, copper roof |annual_festival = {{ill|October 10|ja|10月10日|1=October 10|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2921}} |cultural_property = |ISO = JP-29 |lat_d = 34|lat_m = 40|lat_s = 21.05 |lon_d = 135|lon_m = 49|lon_s = 45.00 |map = Nara city |location = {{ill|Nara|WD=Q169134}} |coordinates = {{Coord|34.67252777777778|N|135.82916666666668|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Asuka Shrine''' (Asuka Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|1=Shinto shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q845945}} located in {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|1=Nara Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131287}}, {{ill|Nara (city)|ja|奈良市|1=Nara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q169134}}, {{ill|Hokukyōbatechō|ja|北京終町}}. It is commonly referred to as '''Kyōbate Tenjinsha''', and also known as '''Kōbaiden Shrine''' and '''Heijōza Asuka Shrine'''{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples Edition|page=147}}{{sfn|Nara Prefectural History 5: Shrines|page=268}}. == Deities == According to the Nara City History, the shrine enshrines the following five kami{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples Edition|page=147}}, although there are alternate traditions{{Refnest|group="Note"|According to a wooden information board on the grounds, the five kami are:
* {{ill|Asuka Sanbime no Mikoto|ja|明日香三比女命}}
* {{ill|Ame no Kotoshironushi|ja|天之事代主命}}
* {{ill|Kayanarumi no Mikoto|ja|賀夜奈留美命}}
* {{ill|Usutaki-hime no Mikoto|ja|宇須太岐比女命}}
* {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|贈太政大臣道眞公|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}Wooden board on-site}}. * {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神|1=Kotoshironushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8009611}} * {{ill|Kayanarumi no Mikoto|ja|加夜奈留美命}} * {{ill|Usutaki-hime no Mikoto|ja|宇須多岐比女命}} * {{ill|Fusokuru-hime no Mikoto|ja|不足留比女命}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|菅原道真|1=Sugawara no Michizane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} == History == === Origins === According to a document titled "Document Showing Shrine Origins and History," attached to the official 1953 registration under the Religious Corporations ActDocument Showing Shrine Origins and History: {{Quotation|In the 8th month of the 2nd year of {{ill|Yōrō|ja|養老|1=Yōrō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1373061}} ({{Refnest|group="Note"|According to a wooden board on the premises, the transfer occurred in {{ill|Wadō 3|ja|和銅3年}} ({{ill|711|ja|711年|1=711|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q23519}}).}}), the enshrined deity of Asuka Shrine in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|飛鳥村|1=Asuka, Nara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11665974}}, Takaichi District, Yamato Province, was transferred to the hill of Asuka in the Shijō Nanabō (4th Avenue, 7th Ward) of the new capital of Heijō, as the tutelary shrine of the {{ill|Gangō-ji|ja|元興寺|1=Gangō-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1203099}} temple in Nanto. This marked the founding of the present Asuka Shrine. In the medieval period, as Gangō-ji fell into decline, the shrine also diminished and was relocated to a corner of Kyōbate, as it remains today.{{Refnest|group="Note"|According to a wooden board on-site, the relocation to Kyōbate occurred after the {{ill|Bunmei uprisings|ja|土一揆|1=tsuchi-ikki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11422932}} during the {{ill|Hōtoku era|ja|宝徳|1=Hōtoku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1143606}} (15th century) or {{ill|Tenbun era|ja|天文|1=Tenbun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21096812}} (16th century). A paper signboard on the premises says it happened in {{ill|Ōan 2|ja|応安2年}} ({{ill|1369|ja|1369年|1=1369|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6211}})Paper board on-site. The Nara City History adopts the Ōan 2 date{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples Edition|page=147}}.}} In {{ill|Bunsei 9|ja|文政9年}}, {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|菅原道真|1=Sugawara no Michizane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} was added as a deity, and the shrine came to be popularly known as Kōbaiden Shrine. The {{ill|Daijōin|ja|大乗院|1=Daijō-in|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16766994}} abbots of Gangō-ji oversaw its worship. In the Meiji period, it became the ujigami (local tutelary shrine) of the Kyōbate area of Nara City under the name Kōbaiden Shrine. However, the old religious corporation under that name was dissolved and the shrine reverted to its historic name of Asuka Shrine, becoming a new religious corporation under the postwar Religious Corporations Act. The Nara City and Prefectural histories issued thereafter follow this narrative{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples Edition|page=147}}{{sfn|Nara Prefectural History 5: Shrines|page=268}}. === Changes in Records and Deities in the Early Modern Period === However, no early modern or earlier records mention that this shrine moved from Asuka with Gangō-ji. Also, while the origin document claims that Tenjin (Sugawara no Michizane) was enshrined in {{ill|Bunsei 9|ja|文政9年}} ({{ill|1826|ja|1826年|1=1826|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7004}}), older records say Tenjin was enshrined to commemorate Kyōbate Village’s victory in a water dispute in the {{ill|17th century|ja|17世紀|1=17th century|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7016}}{{sfn|Kadokawa Dictionary of Place Names|page=397}}. Early documents and maps show various discrepancies in the shrine's name and enshrined deities: {| class="wikitable" |+ Records of Shrines in Kyōbate Village ! Document !! Period !! Shrine Name !! Deity !! Notes |- | {{ill|Nagakuradouki|ja|長闇堂記|1=Murai Kodō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q96711987}}{{sfn|Kobori Enshū’s Architecture|page=150,158}} || {{ill|Kan'ei 17|ja|寛永17年}} ({{ill|1640|ja|1640年|1=1640|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6901}}) || Kyōbate Town Tenjin || {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|菅原道真|1=Sugawara no Michizane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}Inferred from the shrine name. || The carved stone used as a traffic barrier was taken by {{ill|Nagakuradou|ja|長闇堂}} and later admired by {{ill|Kobori Enshū|ja|小堀遠州|1=Kobori Enshū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q541040}}, who used it in a lantern at his villa and gifted it to {{ill|Tokugawa Hidetada|ja|徳川秀忠|1=Tokugawa Hidetada|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q314464}}. This popularized lanterns with Buddhist carvings{{sfn|Kobori Enshū’s Architecture|page=150,158}}. |- | {{ill|Nara Bak|ja|奈良曝}}{{sfn|Nara Bak|page=39}} || {{ill|Jōkyō 4|ja|貞享4年}} ({{ill|1687|ja|1687年|1=1687|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7668}}) || Fuji Gongen || {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|伊勢}}, {{ill|Kasuga|ja|春日|1=Kasuga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q446420}}, {{ill|Fuji Gongen|ja|富士権現}} || Originally enshrined Amaterasu and Kasuga, but Fuji was added after a victory in a water dispute with {{ill|Kanōon Village|ja|鹿野園村}} in {{ill|Keichō 18|ja|慶長18年}} ({{ill|1613|ja|1613年|1=1613|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6776}}){{Refnest|group="Note"|name="suiron"|The legal complaint from that time appears in the {{ill|Honkō Kokushi Diary|ja|本光国師日記|1=Honkō Kokushi Nikki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11519861}}, entry for {{ill|Keichō 19|ja|慶長19年|1=1614|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6781}}, 6th month, 12th day{{sfn|Kadokawa Dictionary of Place Names|page=397}}{{sfn|Honkō Diary|page=97}}. The dispute opponent is listed as Takabatake Village{{sfn|Honkō Diary|page=97}}.}}. |- | Nara Town Map, Twelve-fold book{{sfn|Nara Exhibition Catalog|page=1}} || ca. {{ill|Hōei|ja|宝永|1=Hōei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1145262}}–{{ill|Shōtoku (Japanese era)|ja|正徳|1=正徳|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11123425}} ({{ill|1704–1715|ja|1704年–1715年}}) || Kasuga Shrine || KasugaInferred from name. || - |- | {{ill|Narabōmoku Sakkai|ja|奈良坊目拙解|1=Murai Kodō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q96711987}}{{sfn|Narabōmoku Sakkai|page=33}} || {{ill|Kyōhō 15|ja|享保15年}} ({{ill|1730|ja|1730年|1=1730|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6957}}) || Tenjinsha || {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|菅原道真|1=Sugawara no Michizane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}, {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫}}, {{ill|Ōyama-tsumi|ja|大山祇神|1=Ōyamatsumi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q386563}} || Enshrinement of Tenjin after water dispute victory over Takabatake Village. Shrine destroyed in a fire on {{ill|May 6, 1706|ja|宝永3年5月6日}}, all relics and records lost. |- | {{ill|Yamato Meisho Zue|ja|大和名所図絵}}{{sfn|Yamato Meisho Zue|page=132}} || {{ill|Kansei 3|ja|寛政3年}} ({{ill|1791|ja|1791年|1=1791|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7784}}) || Fuji Gongen Shrine || Ise, Kasuga, Fuji Gongen || Initially Ise and Kasuga, Fuji added during Keichō era. |- | {{ill|Nara Saikenzu|ja|奈良細見図}}{{sfn|Nara Saikenzu}} || {{ill|1889|ja|1889年|1=1889|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7831}} ({{ill|Meiji 22|ja|明治22年|1=1889|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7831}}) || Kōbaiden Shrine || Sugawara no MichizaneInferred from association with Kōbaiden, the residence of Sugawara no Michizane. || - |- | Field Survey Nara City Map{{sfn|Nara City Map}} || {{ill|1917|ja|大正6年|1=1917|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2092}} || Kōbaiden Shrine || Sugawara no Michizane || - |- | Official Registration History || {{ill|1953|ja|昭和28年|1=1953|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18585}} || Asuka Shrine || Gangō-ji’s tutelary deity (unspecified)
Sugawara no Michizane || Transferred from Asuka to Heijō with Gangō-ji; moved to Kyōbate in medieval period. Michizane added in 1826. |- | {{ill|Nara Town Topography|ja|奈良町風土記}}{{sfn|Nara Town Topography|page=76}} || {{ill|1976|ja|昭和51年|1=1976|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2480}} || Kōbai Tenjinsha || Sugawara no Michizane
Konohanasakuya-hime, Ōyama-tsumi || Fuji deities are auxiliary shrines. Recently renamed Asuka Shrine. |- | Nara City History{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples Edition|page=147}} || {{ill|1985|ja|昭和60年|1=1985|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2431}} || Asuka Shrine || Kotoshironushi, Kayanarumi, Usutaki-hime, Fusokuru-hime, Sugawara no Michizane || Listed as Asuka Shrine in 1977 registry. |} === Similar Origins at Nearby Shrines === The tradition that this shrine was the tutelary shrine of Gangō-ji transferred from Asuka during the establishment of Heijō-kyō is also found in Edo-period records regarding nearby shrines such as {{ill|Isagawa Shrine (Nara City Nishishinmachi)|ja|率川神社 (奈良市西新屋町)|1=Isagawa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q48761131}} about 400 meters north in {{ill|Nishishinmachi, Nara|ja|西新屋町|1=Nishinoshin'yachō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28686454}}, and {{ill|Hakusan Shrine (Nara City Gangoji Town)|ja|白山神社 (奈良市元興寺町)|1=Hakusan Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q48761897}} in {{ill|Gangoji Town|ja|元興寺町|1=Gangōji-chō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28687279}}{{sfn|Narabōmoku Sakkai|p=22,58}}{{sfn|Gangoji Origin Record|p=153–155}}. However, no sources confirm these shrines moved to Kyōbate. == Shrine Grounds == [[File:Nara Kyobate Temjin Honden1.jpg|thumb|150px|Main Hall]] [[File:Kasuga-taisha sanjyuhassho jinja.jpg|thumb|150px|Reference: Kasuga Shrine Sub-shrine Sanjūhassho Shrine]] [[File:Kasuganocho, Nara, Nara Prefecture 630-8212, Japan - panoramio - jetsun (4).jpg|thumb|150px|Reference: Kasuga Shrine Sub-shrine Mizutani Shrine]] === Main Hall === The main hall was relocated from a former sub-shrine of {{ill|Kasuga Shrine|ja|春日大社|1=Kasuga-taisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q714559}} in a practice known as “Kasuga Utsushi.” There are two theories about which sub-shrine was moved: one identifies it as the {{ill|Sanjūhassho Shrine|ja|三十八所神社}}{{sfn|Nara Prefectural History 8: Architecture|page=912}}, the other as {{ill|Mizutani Shrine (Nara City)|ja|水谷神社|1=Mizutani Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11549203}}{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples Edition|page=147}}{{sfn|Nara Prefectural History 5: Shrines|page=268}}.As of 2018, structural comparisons such as pillar placement suggest resemblance to Sanjūhassho Shrine. If it is Mizutani Shrine, the relocation is thought to have occurred in {{ill|Bunsei 9|ja|文政9年}} ({{ill|1826|ja|1826年|1=1826|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7004}}){{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples Edition|page=147}}{{sfn|Nara Prefectural History 5: Shrines|page=268}}. The structure is a {{ill|Sangensha-zukuri|ja|三間社造}} with a copper-sheet roof{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples Edition|page=147}}. Decorative elements such as kenbamon (sword-hilt crests) are seen on the lintels{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples Edition|page=147}}. === Sub-shrines === * {{ill|Munakata Shrine|ja|宗像神社|1=Munakata shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11451876}} (Deity: {{ill|Ichikishimahime no Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishimahime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11059454}}) – formerly located within the grounds of {{ill|Kōdaiji (Kyoto)|ja|高台寺|1=Kōdai-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2702064}} in Hanazono{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples Edition|page=147}}{{sfn|Nara Prefectural History 5: Shrines|page=268}}. * Ōkuninushi Shrine (Deities: {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命}}, {{ill|Hiruko|ja|蛭子命|1=Hiruko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1067028}}) – formerly within {{ill|Gokurakubō|ja|禅定院（極楽坊）}} of Gangō-ji{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples Edition|page=147}}{{sfn|Nara Prefectural History 5: Shrines|page=268}}. * {{ill|Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社|1=Kotohira shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11646488}} (Deity: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命}}) – formerly at {{ill|Shōtōin|ja|小塔院|1=Shōtō-in Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11459543}}{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples Edition|page=147}}{{sfn|Nara Prefectural History 5: Shrines|page=268}}.As of 2018, the enshrined deity is indicated as Konohanasakuya-hime. * {{ill|Tsukihara Myōjinsha|ja|月原明神社}} – A small shrine once by the pond called Amida-ike; relocated in {{ill|1982|ja|昭和57年|1=1982|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2436}}, June, by the Kyōbate Agricultural Union{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples Edition|page=147}}. * {{ill|Shichibenten Shrine|ja|七弁天社}} – Also relocated from the bank of Amida-ike{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples Edition|page=147}}. Other enshrined deities include {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|ja|崇徳天皇|1=Emperor Sutoku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q349287}}{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples Edition|page=147}}. == [[sn:commons:Category:Kyobate Tenjinsha]][[ja:飛鳥神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q22117766]] Wikidata name: Asuka Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:47, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[ja:飛鳥神社]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Asuka Shrine (Kyōbate Tenjinsha) |image = File:Nara Kyobate Temjin Honden1.jpg |location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|1=Nara Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131287}}{{ill|Nara (city)|ja|奈良市|1=Nara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q169134}} Hokukyōbatechō 45 |deity = {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神|1=Kotoshironushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8009611}}
{{ill|Kayanarumi no Mikoto|ja|加夜奈留美命}}
{{ill|Usutaki-hime no Mikoto|ja|宇須多岐比女命}}
{{ill|Fusokuru-hime no Mikoto|ja|不足留比女命}}
{{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|菅原道真|1=Sugawara no Michizane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} |rank = |founded = |honden = Sangensha-zukuri, copper roof |annual_festival = {{ill|October 10|ja|10月10日|1=October 10|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2921}} |cultural_property = |ISO = JP-29 |lat_d = 34|lat_m = 40|lat_s = 21.05 |lon_d = 135|lon_m = 49|lon_s = 45.00 |map = Nara city |location = {{ill|Nara|WD=Q169134}} |coordinates = {{Coord|34.67252777777778|N|135.82916666666668|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Asuka Shrine''' (Asuka Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|1=Shinto shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q845945}} located in {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|1=Nara Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131287}}, {{ill|Nara (city)|ja|奈良市|1=Nara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q169134}}, {{ill|Hokukyōbatechō|ja|北京終町}}. It is commonly referred to as '''Kyōbate Tenjinsha''', and also known as '''Kōbaiden Shrine''' and '''Heijōza Asuka Shrine'''{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples Edition|page=147}}{{sfn|Nara Prefectural History 5: Shrines|page=268}}. == Deities == According to the Nara City History, the shrine enshrines the following five kami{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples Edition|page=147}}, although there are alternate traditions{{Refnest|group="Note"|According to a wooden information board on the grounds, the five kami are:
* {{ill|Asuka Sanbime no Mikoto|ja|明日香三比女命}}
* {{ill|Ame no Kotoshironushi|ja|天之事代主命}}
* {{ill|Kayanarumi no Mikoto|ja|賀夜奈留美命}}
* {{ill|Usutaki-hime no Mikoto|ja|宇須太岐比女命}}
* {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|贈太政大臣道眞公|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}Wooden board on-site}}. * {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神|1=Kotoshironushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8009611}} * {{ill|Kayanarumi no Mikoto|ja|加夜奈留美命}} * {{ill|Usutaki-hime no Mikoto|ja|宇須多岐比女命}} * {{ill|Fusokuru-hime no Mikoto|ja|不足留比女命}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|菅原道真|1=Sugawara no Michizane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} == History == === Origins === According to a document titled "Document Showing Shrine Origins and History," attached to the official 1953 registration under the Religious Corporations ActDocument Showing Shrine Origins and History: {{Quotation|In the 8th month of the 2nd year of {{ill|Yōrō|ja|養老|1=Yōrō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1373061}} ({{Refnest|group="Note"|According to a wooden board on the premises, the transfer occurred in {{ill|Wadō 3|ja|和銅3年}} ({{ill|711|ja|711年|1=711|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q23519}}).}}), the enshrined deity of Asuka Shrine in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|飛鳥村|1=Asuka, Nara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11665974}}, Takaichi District, Yamato Province, was transferred to the hill of Asuka in the Shijō Nanabō (4th Avenue, 7th Ward) of the new capital of Heijō, as the tutelary shrine of the {{ill|Gangō-ji|ja|元興寺|1=Gangō-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1203099}} temple in Nanto. This marked the founding of the present Asuka Shrine. In the medieval period, as Gangō-ji fell into decline, the shrine also diminished and was relocated to a corner of Kyōbate, as it remains today.{{Refnest|group="Note"|According to a wooden board on-site, the relocation to Kyōbate occurred after the {{ill|Bunmei uprisings|ja|土一揆|1=tsuchi-ikki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11422932}} during the {{ill|Hōtoku era|ja|宝徳|1=Hōtoku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1143606}} (15th century) or {{ill|Tenbun era|ja|天文|1=Tenbun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21096812}} (16th century). A paper signboard on the premises says it happened in {{ill|Ōan 2|ja|応安2年}} ({{ill|1369|ja|1369年|1=1369|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6211}})Paper board on-site. The Nara City History adopts the Ōan 2 date{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples Edition|page=147}}.}} In {{ill|Bunsei 9|ja|文政9年}}, {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|菅原道真|1=Sugawara no Michizane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} was added as a deity, and the shrine came to be popularly known as Kōbaiden Shrine. The {{ill|Daijōin|ja|大乗院|1=Daijō-in|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16766994}} abbots of Gangō-ji oversaw its worship. In the Meiji period, it became the ujigami (local tutelary shrine) of the Kyōbate area of Nara City under the name Kōbaiden Shrine. However, the old religious corporation under that name was dissolved and the shrine reverted to its historic name of Asuka Shrine, becoming a new religious corporation under the postwar Religious Corporations Act. The Nara City and Prefectural histories issued thereafter follow this narrative{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples Edition|page=147}}{{sfn|Nara Prefectural History 5: Shrines|page=268}}. === Changes in Records and Deities in the Early Modern Period === However, no early modern or earlier records mention that this shrine moved from Asuka with Gangō-ji. Also, while the origin document claims that Tenjin (Sugawara no Michizane) was enshrined in {{ill|Bunsei 9|ja|文政9年}} ({{ill|1826|ja|1826年|1=1826|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7004}}), older records say Tenjin was enshrined to commemorate Kyōbate Village’s victory in a water dispute in the {{ill|17th century|ja|17世紀|1=17th century|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7016}}{{sfn|Kadokawa Dictionary of Place Names|page=397}}. Early documents and maps show various discrepancies in the shrine's name and enshrined deities: {| class="wikitable" |+ Records of Shrines in Kyōbate Village ! Document !! Period !! Shrine Name !! Deity !! Notes |- | {{ill|Nagakuradouki|ja|長闇堂記|1=Murai Kodō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q96711987}}{{sfn|Kobori Enshū’s Architecture|page=150,158}} || {{ill|Kan'ei 17|ja|寛永17年}} ({{ill|1640|ja|1640年|1=1640|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6901}}) || Kyōbate Town Tenjin || {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|菅原道真|1=Sugawara no Michizane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}Inferred from the shrine name. || The carved stone used as a traffic barrier was taken by {{ill|Nagakuradou|ja|長闇堂}} and later admired by {{ill|Kobori Enshū|ja|小堀遠州|1=Kobori Enshū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q541040}}, who used it in a lantern at his villa and gifted it to {{ill|Tokugawa Hidetada|ja|徳川秀忠|1=Tokugawa Hidetada|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q314464}}. This popularized lanterns with Buddhist carvings{{sfn|Kobori Enshū’s Architecture|page=150,158}}. |- | {{ill|Nara Bak|ja|奈良曝}}{{sfn|Nara Bak|page=39}} || {{ill|Jōkyō 4|ja|貞享4年}} ({{ill|1687|ja|1687年|1=1687|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7668}}) || Fuji Gongen || {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|伊勢}}, {{ill|Kasuga|ja|春日|1=Kasuga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q446420}}, {{ill|Fuji Gongen|ja|富士権現}} || Originally enshrined Amaterasu and Kasuga, but Fuji was added after a victory in a water dispute with {{ill|Kanōon Village|ja|鹿野園村}} in {{ill|Keichō 18|ja|慶長18年}} ({{ill|1613|ja|1613年|1=1613|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6776}}){{Refnest|group="Note"|name="suiron"|The legal complaint from that time appears in the {{ill|Honkō Kokushi Diary|ja|本光国師日記|1=Honkō Kokushi Nikki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11519861}}, entry for {{ill|Keichō 19|ja|慶長19年|1=1614|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6781}}, 6th month, 12th day{{sfn|Kadokawa Dictionary of Place Names|page=397}}{{sfn|Honkō Diary|page=97}}. The dispute opponent is listed as Takabatake Village{{sfn|Honkō Diary|page=97}}.}}. |- | Nara Town Map, Twelve-fold book{{sfn|Nara Exhibition Catalog|page=1}} || ca. {{ill|Hōei|ja|宝永|1=Hōei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1145262}}–{{ill|Shōtoku (Japanese era)|ja|正徳|1=正徳|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11123425}} ({{ill|1704–1715|ja|1704年–1715年}}) || Kasuga Shrine || KasugaInferred from name. || - |- | {{ill|Narabōmoku Sakkai|ja|奈良坊目拙解|1=Murai Kodō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q96711987}}{{sfn|Narabōmoku Sakkai|page=33}} || {{ill|Kyōhō 15|ja|享保15年}} ({{ill|1730|ja|1730年|1=1730|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6957}}) || Tenjinsha || {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|菅原道真|1=Sugawara no Michizane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}, {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫}}, {{ill|Ōyama-tsumi|ja|大山祇神|1=Ōyamatsumi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q386563}} || Enshrinement of Tenjin after water dispute victory over Takabatake Village. Shrine destroyed in a fire on {{ill|May 6, 1706|ja|宝永3年5月6日}}, all relics and records lost. |- | {{ill|Yamato Meisho Zue|ja|大和名所図絵}}{{sfn|Yamato Meisho Zue|page=132}} || {{ill|Kansei 3|ja|寛政3年}} ({{ill|1791|ja|1791年|1=1791|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7784}}) || Fuji Gongen Shrine || Ise, Kasuga, Fuji Gongen || Initially Ise and Kasuga, Fuji added during Keichō era. |- | {{ill|Nara Saikenzu|ja|奈良細見図}}{{sfn|Nara Saikenzu}} || {{ill|1889|ja|1889年|1=1889|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7831}} ({{ill|Meiji 22|ja|明治22年|1=1889|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7831}}) || Kōbaiden Shrine || Sugawara no MichizaneInferred from association with Kōbaiden, the residence of Sugawara no Michizane. || - |- | Field Survey Nara City Map{{sfn|Nara City Map}} || {{ill|1917|ja|大正6年|1=1917|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2092}} || Kōbaiden Shrine || Sugawara no Michizane || - |- | Official Registration History || {{ill|1953|ja|昭和28年|1=1953|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18585}} || Asuka Shrine || Gangō-ji’s tutelary deity (unspecified)
Sugawara no Michizane || Transferred from Asuka to Heijō with Gangō-ji; moved to Kyōbate in medieval period. Michizane added in 1826. |- | {{ill|Nara Town Topography|ja|奈良町風土記}}{{sfn|Nara Town Topography|page=76}} || {{ill|1976|ja|昭和51年|1=1976|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2480}} || Kōbai Tenjinsha || Sugawara no Michizane
Konohanasakuya-hime, Ōyama-tsumi || Fuji deities are auxiliary shrines. Recently renamed Asuka Shrine. |- | Nara City History{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples Edition|page=147}} || {{ill|1985|ja|昭和60年|1=1985|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2431}} || Asuka Shrine || Kotoshironushi, Kayanarumi, Usutaki-hime, Fusokuru-hime, Sugawara no Michizane || Listed as Asuka Shrine in 1977 registry. |} === Similar Origins at Nearby Shrines === The tradition that this shrine was the tutelary shrine of Gangō-ji transferred from Asuka during the establishment of Heijō-kyō is also found in Edo-period records regarding nearby shrines such as {{ill|Isagawa Shrine (Nara City Nishishinmachi)|ja|率川神社 (奈良市西新屋町)|1=Isagawa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q48761131}} about 400 meters north in {{ill|Nishishinmachi, Nara|ja|西新屋町|1=Nishinoshin'yachō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28686454}}, and {{ill|Hakusan Shrine (Nara City Gangoji Town)|ja|白山神社 (奈良市元興寺町)|1=Hakusan Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q48761897}} in {{ill|Gangoji Town|ja|元興寺町|1=Gangōji-chō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28687279}}{{sfn|Narabōmoku Sakkai|p=22,58}}{{sfn|Gangoji Origin Record|p=153–155}}. However, no sources confirm these shrines moved to Kyōbate. == Shrine Grounds == [[File:Nara Kyobate Temjin Honden1.jpg|thumb|150px|Main Hall]] [[File:Kasuga-taisha sanjyuhassho jinja.jpg|thumb|150px|Reference: Kasuga Shrine Sub-shrine Sanjūhassho Shrine]] [[File:Kasuganocho, Nara, Nara Prefecture 630-8212, Japan - panoramio - jetsun (4).jpg|thumb|150px|Reference: Kasuga Shrine Sub-shrine Mizutani Shrine]] === Main Hall === The main hall was relocated from a former sub-shrine of {{ill|Kasuga Shrine|ja|春日大社|1=Kasuga-taisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q714559}} in a practice known as “Kasuga Utsushi.” There are two theories about which sub-shrine was moved: one identifies it as the {{ill|Sanjūhassho Shrine|ja|三十八所神社}}{{sfn|Nara Prefectural History 8: Architecture|page=912}}, the other as {{ill|Mizutani Shrine (Nara City)|ja|水谷神社|1=Mizutani Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11549203}}{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples Edition|page=147}}{{sfn|Nara Prefectural History 5: Shrines|page=268}}.As of 2018, structural comparisons such as pillar placement suggest resemblance to Sanjūhassho Shrine. If it is Mizutani Shrine, the relocation is thought to have occurred in {{ill|Bunsei 9|ja|文政9年}} ({{ill|1826|ja|1826年|1=1826|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7004}}){{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples Edition|page=147}}{{sfn|Nara Prefectural History 5: Shrines|page=268}}. The structure is a {{ill|Sangensha-zukuri|ja|三間社造}} with a copper-sheet roof{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples Edition|page=147}}. Decorative elements such as kenbamon (sword-hilt crests) are seen on the lintels{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples Edition|page=147}}. === Sub-shrines === * {{ill|Munakata Shrine|ja|宗像神社|1=Munakata shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11451876}} (Deity: {{ill|Ichikishimahime no Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishimahime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11059454}}) – formerly located within the grounds of {{ill|Kōdaiji (Kyoto)|ja|高台寺|1=Kōdai-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2702064}} in Hanazono{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples Edition|page=147}}{{sfn|Nara Prefectural History 5: Shrines|page=268}}. * Ōkuninushi Shrine (Deities: {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命}}, {{ill|Hiruko|ja|蛭子命|1=Hiruko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1067028}}) – formerly within {{ill|Gokurakubō|ja|禅定院（極楽坊）}} of Gangō-ji{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples Edition|page=147}}{{sfn|Nara Prefectural History 5: Shrines|page=268}}. * {{ill|Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社|1=Kotohira shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11646488}} (Deity: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命}}) – formerly at {{ill|Shōtōin|ja|小塔院|1=Shōtō-in Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11459543}}{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples Edition|page=147}}{{sfn|Nara Prefectural History 5: Shrines|page=268}}.As of 2018, the enshrined deity is indicated as Konohanasakuya-hime. * {{ill|Tsukihara Myōjinsha|ja|月原明神社}} – A small shrine once by the pond called Amida-ike; relocated in {{ill|1982|ja|昭和57年|1=1982|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2436}}, June, by the Kyōbate Agricultural Union{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples Edition|page=147}}. * {{ill|Shichibenten Shrine|ja|七弁天社}} – Also relocated from the bank of Amida-ike{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples Edition|page=147}}. Other enshrined deities include {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|ja|崇徳天皇|1=Emperor Sutoku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q349287}}{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples Edition|page=147}}. == Practical Information == ; Address * 45 Hokukyōbatechō, {{ill|Nara (city)|ja|奈良市|1=Nara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q169134}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|1=Nara Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131287}} ; Access * Nearest bus stop: About 20 minutes on foot from {{ill|Nara Station|ja|奈良駅|1=Nara Station|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1150978}} on the {{ill|Yamatoji Line|ja|大和路線|1=Yamatoji Line|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1151659}} == Notes == === Annotations === {{Reflist|group="Note"|2}} === Shrines|editor1-first=Genta|editor1-last=Ikeda|editor2-first=Toshikazu|editor2-last=Miyasaka|publisher={{ill|Meicho Publishing|ja|名著出版}}|year=1989|isbn=462601335X|ref={{sfnref|Nara Prefectural History 5: Shrines}}}} * {{Cite book|title=Nara Prefectural History 8: Architecture|editor=Nara Prefectural History Editorial Committee|publisher=Meicho Publishing|year=1998|ref={{sfnref|Nara Prefectural History 8: Architecture}}}} * {{Cite book|title=Nara City History: Shrines and Temples Edition|editor=Nara City History Editorial Council|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|1=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4346755}}|year=1985|ref={{sfnref|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples Edition}}}} * {{Cite book|title=Kadokawa Japanese Place Name Dictionary 29 Nara Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Kadokawa Shoten|ja|角川書店|1=Kadokawa Shoten|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1054765}}|year=1990|ref={{sfnref|Kadokawa Dictionary of Place Names}}}} * {{Cite Kodō|editor=Kitanoya Tokutoshi (trans.)|publisher=Sōgeisha|year=1977|ref={{sfnref|Narabōmoku Sakkai}}}} * {{Cite book|title=Yamato Meisho Zue|editor=Meisho Zue Publication Committee|publisher=Meisho Zue Publication Committee|year=1919|ref={{sfnref|Yamato Meisho Zue}}}} * {{Cite book|title=Kobori Enshū’s Architecture|author=Mori Omo|publisher=Nara National Research Institute for Cultural Properties|year=1966|ref={{sfnref|Kobori Enshū’s Architecture}}}} * {{Cite book|title=Nara Town Topography|author=Yamada Kumao|publisher=Toyozumi Shoten|year=1976|ref={{sfnref|Nara Town Topography}}}}
