[[co:commons:Category:Kyobate Tenjinsha]][[ja:飛鳥神社]] [[co:commons:Category:Kyobate Tenjinsha]][[ja:飛鳥神社]] [[ja:飛鳥神社]] [[ja:飛鳥神社]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Asuka Shrine (Kyōbate Tenjinsha) |image = File:Nara Kyobate Temjin Honden1.jpg |location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|1=Nara Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q131287}}{{ill|Nara (city)|ja|奈良市|1=Nara (city)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q169134}} Hokukyōbatechō 45 |deity = {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q8009611}}
{{ill|Kayanarumi no Mikoto|ja|加夜奈留美命}}
{{ill|Usutaki-hime no Mikoto|ja|宇須多岐比女命}}
{{ill|Fusokuru-hime no Mikoto|ja|不足留比女命}}
{{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|菅原道真|1=Sugawara no Michizane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q382005}} |rank = |founded = |honden = Sangensha-zukuri, copper roof |annual_festival = {{ill|October 10|ja|10月10日|1=October 10|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q2921}} |cultural_property = |ISO = JP-29 |lat_d = 34|lat_m = 40|lat_s = 21.05 |lon_d = 135|lon_m = 49|lon_s = 45.00 |map = Nara city |location = {{ill|Nara|qid=Q169134|1=Nara (city)}} |coordinates = {{Coord|34.67252777777778|N|135.82916666666668|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Asuka Shrine''' (Asuka Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|1=Shinto shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q845945}} located in {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|1=Nara Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q131287}}, {{ill|Nara (city)|ja|奈良市|1=Nara (city)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q169134}}, {{ill|Hokukyōbatechō|ja|北京終町}}. It is commonly referred to as '''Kyōbate Tenjinsha''', and also known as '''Kōbaiden Shrine''' and '''Heijōza Asuka Shrine'''{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples Edition|page=147}}{{sfn|Nara Prefectural History 5: Shrines|page=268}}. == Deities == According to the Nara City History, the shrine enshrines the following five kami{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples Edition|page=147}}, although there are alternate traditions{{Refnest|group="Note"|According to a wooden information board on the grounds, the five kami are:
* {{ill|Asuka Sanbime no Mikoto|ja|明日香三比女命}}
* {{ill|Ame no Kotoshironushi|ja|天之事代主命}}
* {{ill|Kayanarumi no Mikoto|ja|賀夜奈留美命}}
* {{ill|Usutaki-hime no Mikoto|ja|宇須太岐比女命}}
* {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|贈太政大臣道眞公|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q382005}}Wooden board on-site}}. * {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q8009611}} * {{ill|Kayanarumi no Mikoto|ja|加夜奈留美命}} * {{ill|Usutaki-hime no Mikoto|ja|宇須多岐比女命}} * {{ill|Fusokuru-hime no Mikoto|ja|不足留比女命}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|菅原道真|1=Sugawara no Michizane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q382005}} == History == === Origins === According to a document titled "Document Showing Shrine Origins and History," attached to the official 1953 registration under the Religious Corporations ActDocument Showing Shrine Origins and History: {{Quotation|In the 8th month of the 2nd year of {{ill|Yōrō|ja|養老|1=Yōrō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1373061}} ({{Refnest|group="Note"|According to a wooden board on the premises, the transfer occurred in {{ill|Wadō 3|ja|和銅3年}} ({{ill|711|ja|711年|1=AD 711|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q23519}}).}}), the enshrined deity of Asuka Shrine in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|飛鳥村|1=Q11665974|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11665974}}, Takaichi District, Yamato Province, was transferred to the hill of Asuka in the Shijō Nanabō (4th Avenue, 7th Ward) of the new capital of Heijō, as the tutelary shrine of the {{ill|Gangō-ji|ja|元興寺|1=Gangō-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1203099}} temple in Nanto. This marked the founding of the present Asuka Shrine. In the medieval period, as Gangō-ji fell into decline, the shrine also diminished and was relocated to a corner of Kyōbate, as it remains today.{{Refnest|group="Note"|According to a wooden board on-site, the relocation to Kyōbate occurred after the {{ill|Bunmei uprisings|ja|土一揆|1=Tsuchi Ikki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11422932}} during the {{ill|Hōtoku era|ja|宝徳|1=Hōtoku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1143606}} (15th century) or {{ill|Tenbun era|ja|天文|1=Tenmon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q21096812}} (16th century). A paper signboard on the premises says it happened in {{ill|Ōan 2|ja|応安2年}} ({{ill|1369|ja|1369年|1=1369|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q6211}})Paper board on-site. The Nara City History adopts the Ōan 2 date{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples Edition|page=147}}.}} In {{ill|Bunsei 9|ja|文政9年}}, {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|菅原道真|1=Sugawara no Michizane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q382005}} was added as a deity, and the shrine came to be popularly known as Kōbaiden Shrine. The {{ill|Daijōin|ja|大乗院|1=Daijō-in|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q16766994}} abbots of Gangō-ji oversaw its worship. In the Meiji period, it became the ujigami (local tutelary shrine) of the Kyōbate area of Nara City under the name Kōbaiden Shrine. However, the old religious corporation under that name was dissolved and the shrine reverted to its historic name of Asuka Shrine, becoming a new religious corporation under the postwar Religious Corporations Act. The Nara City and Prefectural histories issued thereafter follow this narrative{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples Edition|page=147}}{{sfn|Nara Prefectural History 5: Shrines|page=268}}. === Changes in Records and Deities in the Early Modern Period === However, no early modern or earlier records mention that this shrine moved from Asuka with Gangō-ji. Also, while the origin document claims that Tenjin (Sugawara no Michizane) was enshrined in {{ill|Bunsei 9|ja|文政9年}} ({{ill|1826|ja|1826年|1=1826|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q7004}}), older records say Tenjin was enshrined to commemorate Kyōbate Village’s victory in a water dispute in the {{ill|17th century|ja|17世紀|1=17th century|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q7016}}{{sfn|Kadokawa Dictionary of Place Names|page=397}}. Early documents and maps show various discrepancies in the shrine's name and enshrined deities: {| class="wikitable" |+ Records of Shrines in Kyōbate Village ! Document !! Period !! Shrine Name !! Deity !! Notes |- | {{ill|Nagakuradouki|ja|長闇堂記|1=Murai Kodō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q96711987}}{{sfn|Kobori Enshū’s Architecture|page=150,158}} || {{ill|Kan'ei 17|ja|寛永17年}} ({{ill|1640|ja|1640年|1=1640|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q6901}}) || Kyōbate Town Tenjin || {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|菅原道真|1=Sugawara no Michizane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q382005}}Inferred from the shrine name. || The carved stone used as a traffic barrier was taken by {{ill|Nagakuradou|ja|長闇堂}} and later admired by {{ill|Kobori Enshū|ja|小堀遠州|1=Kobori Enshū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q541040}}, who used it in a lantern at his villa and gifted it to {{ill|Tokugawa Hidetada|ja|徳川秀忠|1=Tokugawa Hidetada|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q314464}}. This popularized lanterns with Buddhist carvings{{sfn|Kobori Enshū’s Architecture|page=150,158}}. |- | {{ill|Nara Bak|ja|奈良曝}}{{sfn|Nara Bak|page=39}} || {{ill|Jōkyō 4|ja|貞享4年}} ({{ill|1687|ja|1687年|1=1687|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q7668}}) || Fuji Gongen || {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|伊勢}}, {{ill|Kasuga|ja|春日|1=Kasuga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q446420}}, {{ill|Fuji Gongen|ja|富士権現}} || Originally enshrined Amaterasu and Kasuga, but Fuji was added after a victory in a water dispute with {{ill|Kanōon Village|ja|鹿野園村}} in {{ill|Keichō 18|ja|慶長18年}} ({{ill|1613|ja|1613年|1=1613|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q6776}}){{Refnest|group="Note"|name="suiron"|The legal complaint from that time appears in the {{ill|Honkō Kokushi Diary|ja|本光国師日記|1=Honkō Kokushi Nikki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11519861}}, entry for {{ill|Keichō 19|ja|慶長19年|1=1614|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q6781}}, 6th month, 12th day{{sfn|Kadokawa Dictionary of Place Names|page=397}}{{sfn|Honkō Diary|page=97}}. The dispute opponent is listed as Takabatake Village{{sfn|Honkō Diary|page=97}}.}}. |- | Nara Town Map, Twelve-fold book{{sfn|Nara Exhibition Catalog|page=1}} || ca. {{ill|Hōei|ja|宝永|1=Hōei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1145262}}–{{ill|Shōtoku (Japanese era)|ja|正徳|1=正德|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11123425}} ({{ill|1704–1715|ja|1704年–1715年}}) || Kasuga Shrine || KasugaInferred from name. || - |- | {{ill|Narabōmoku Sakkai|ja|奈良坊目拙解|1=Murai Kodō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q96711987}}{{sfn|Narabōmoku Sakkai|page=33}} || {{ill|Kyōhō 15|ja|享保15年}} ({{ill|1730|ja|1730年|1=1730|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q6957}}) || Tenjinsha || {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|菅原道真|1=Sugawara no Michizane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q382005}}, {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫}}, {{ill|Ōyama-tsumi|ja|大山祇神|1=Ōyamatsumi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q386563}} || Enshrinement of Tenjin after water dispute victory over Takabatake Village. Shrine destroyed in a fire on {{ill|May 6, 1706|ja|宝永3年5月6日}}, all relics and records lost. |- | {{ill|Yamato Meisho Zue|ja|大和名所図絵}}{{sfn|Yamato Meisho Zue|page=132}} || {{ill|Kansei 3|ja|寛政3年}} ({{ill|1791|ja|1791年|1=1791|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q7784}}) || Fuji Gongen Shrine || Ise, Kasuga, Fuji Gongen || Initially Ise and Kasuga, Fuji added during Keichō era. |- | {{ill|Nara Saikenzu|ja|奈良細見図}}{{sfn|Nara Saikenzu}} || {{ill|1889|ja|1889年|1=1889|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q7831}} ({{ill|Meiji 22|ja|明治22年|1=1889|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q7831}}) || Kōbaiden Shrine || Sugawara no MichizaneInferred from association with Kōbaiden, the residence of Sugawara no Michizane. || - |- | Field Survey Nara City Map{{sfn|Nara City Map}} || {{ill|1917|ja|大正6年|1=1917|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q2092}} || Kōbaiden Shrine || Sugawara no Michizane || - |- | Official Registration History || {{ill|1953|ja|昭和28年|1=1953|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q18585}} || Asuka Shrine || Gangō-ji’s tutelary deity (unspecified)
Sugawara no Michizane || Transferred from Asuka to Heijō with Gangō-ji; moved to Kyōbate in medieval period. Michizane added in 1826. |- | {{ill|Nara Town Topography|ja|奈良町風土記}}{{sfn|Nara Town Topography|page=76}} || {{ill|1976|ja|昭和51年|1=1976|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q2480}} || Kōbai Tenjinsha || Sugawara no Michizane
Konohanasakuya-hime, Ōyama-tsumi || Fuji deities are auxiliary shrines. Recently renamed Asuka Shrine. |- | Nara City History{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples Edition|page=147}} || {{ill|1985|ja|昭和60年|1=1985|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q2431}} || Asuka Shrine || Kotoshironushi, Kayanarumi, Usutaki-hime, Fusokuru-hime, Sugawara no Michizane || Listed as Asuka Shrine in 1977 registry. |} === Similar Origins at Nearby Shrines === The tradition that this shrine was the tutelary shrine of Gangō-ji transferred from Asuka during the establishment of Heijō-kyō is also found in Edo-period records regarding nearby shrines such as {{ill|Isagawa Shrine (Nara City Nishishinmachi)|ja|率川神社 (奈良市西新屋町)|1=Isagawa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q48761131}} about 400 meters north in {{ill|Nishishinmachi, Nara|ja|西新屋町|1=Nishinoshin'yachō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q28686454}}, and {{ill|Hakusan Shrine (Nara City Gangoji Town)|ja|白山神社 (奈良市元興寺町)|1=Hakusan Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q48761897}} in {{ill|Gangoji Town|ja|元興寺町|1=Gangōji-chō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q28687279}}{{sfn|Narabōmoku Sakkai|p=22,58}}{{sfn|Gangoji Origin Record|p=153–155}}. However, no sources confirm these shrines moved to Kyōbate. == Shrine Grounds == [[File:Nara Kyobate Temjin Honden1.jpg|thumb|150px|Main Hall]] [[File:Kasuga-taisha sanjyuhassho jinja.jpg|thumb|150px|Reference: Kasuga Shrine Sub-shrine Sanjūhassho Shrine]] [[File:Kasuganocho, Nara, Nara Prefecture 630-8212, Japan - panoramio - jetsun (4).jpg|thumb|150px|Reference: Kasuga Shrine Sub-shrine Mizutani Shrine]] === Main Hall === The main hall was relocated from a former sub-shrine of {{ill|Kasuga Shrine|ja|春日大社|1=Kasuga-taisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q714559}} in a practice known as “Kasuga Utsushi.” There are two theories about which sub-shrine was moved: one identifies it as the {{ill|Sanjūhassho Shrine|ja|三十八所神社}}{{sfn|Nara Prefectural History 8: Architecture|page=912}}, the other as {{ill|Mizutani Shrine (Nara City)|ja|水谷神社|1=Mizutani Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11549203}}{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples Edition|page=147}}{{sfn|Nara Prefectural History 5: Shrines|page=268}}.As of 2018, structural comparisons such as pillar placement suggest resemblance to Sanjūhassho Shrine. If it is Mizutani Shrine, the relocation is thought to have occurred in {{ill|Bunsei 9|ja|文政9年}} ({{ill|1826|ja|1826年|1=1826|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q7004}}){{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples Edition|page=147}}{{sfn|Nara Prefectural History 5: Shrines|page=268}}. The structure is a {{ill|Sangensha-zukuri|ja|三間社造}} with a copper-sheet roof{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples Edition|page=147}}. Decorative elements such as kenbamon (sword-hilt crests) are seen on the lintels{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples Edition|page=147}}. === Sub-shrines === * {{ill|Munakata Shrine|ja|宗像神社|1=Munakata Shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11451876}} (Deity: {{ill|Ichikishimahime no Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11059454}}) – formerly located within the grounds of {{ill|Kōdaiji (Kyoto)|ja|高台寺|1=Kōdai-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q2702064}} in Hanazono{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples Edition|page=147}}{{sfn|Nara Prefectural History 5: Shrines|page=268}}. * Ōkuninushi Shrine (Deities: {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命}}, {{ill|Hiruko|ja|蛭子命|1=Hiruko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1067028}}) – formerly within {{ill|Gokurakubō|ja|禅定院（極楽坊）}} of Gangō-ji{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples Edition|page=147}}{{sfn|Nara Prefectural History 5: Shrines|page=268}}. * {{ill|Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社|1=Kotohira shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11646488}} (Deity: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命}}) – formerly at {{ill|Shōtōin|ja|小塔院|1=Shōtō-in Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11459543}}{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples Edition|page=147}}{{sfn|Nara Prefectural History 5: Shrines|page=268}}.As of 2018, the enshrined deity is indicated as Konohanasakuya-hime. * {{ill|Tsukihara Myōjinsha|ja|月原明神社}} – A small shrine once by the pond called Amida-ike; relocated in {{ill|1982|ja|昭和57年|1=1982|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q2436}}, June, by the Kyōbate Agricultural Union{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples Edition|page=147}}. * {{ill|Shichibenten Shrine|ja|七弁天社}} – Also relocated from the bank of Amida-ike{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples Edition|page=147}}. Other enshrined deities include {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|ja|崇徳天皇|1=Emperor Sutoku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q349287}}{{sfn|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples Edition|page=147}}. == Practical Information == ; Address * 45 Hokukyōbatechō, {{ill|Nara (city)|ja|奈良市|1=Nara (city)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q169134}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|1=Nara Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q131287}} ; Access * Nearest bus stop: About 20 minutes on foot from {{ill|Nara Station|ja|奈良駅|1=Nara Station|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1150978}} on the {{ill|Yamatoji Line|ja|大和路線|1=Yamatoji Line|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1151659}} == Notes == === Annotations === {{Reflist|group="Note"|2}} ===
